title: Nature: Partial Cellular Reprogramming Enters First Human Trial -- A Watershed Moment for Rejuvenation
description: Nature published a major feature on Life Biosciences' ER-100, the first partial epigenetic reprogramming therapy to enter human trials. Using three Yamanaka factors (without c-Myc) delivered via viral vector to the eye, the trial aims to restore vision in glaucoma and NAION patients. Success could open floodgates for organ-level rejuvenation trials across the industry.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences Reports Positive Phase 1 Results for First-in-Class Senotherapeutic RLS-1496
description: Rubedo's GPX4-modulating drug RLS-1496 met its primary endpoint in a Phase 1 trial across psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and skin aging, showing dose-dependent senescent cell clearance, reduced inflammatory cytokines, and 20% epidermal thickness reduction in just four weeks. A Phase 1b/2a actinic keratosis trial is underway in the US.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Congress Concludes in Berlin: Aging Reframed as Loss of Biological Coordination
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity wrapped in Berlin April 8-9, bringing together researchers in mitochondria, microbiota, redox biology, and senescence. The congress established a new paradigm: aging is not a defect to fix but a progressive loss of coordination between biological systems, shifting focus toward system-level resilience.

title: Longevity Biotech Q1 2026 Funding Hits $3.74B -- 56% Year-over-Year Increase Signals Breakout Year
description: Longevity.Technology's DLT dataset shows 49 financing events in Q1 2026 totaling $3.74 billion, a 56% surge over Q1 2025. Deal volume and later-stage rounds are increasing, with a projected full-year range of $8-9 billion. The median deal of $21.8 million suggests a maturing pipeline beyond seed stage.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Major Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin Aging Trial
description: UT Health San Antonio is preparing one of the largest human trials in geroscience, testing rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin in over 700 older adults. Funded by the NIA, the study aims to determine optimal dosing and whether these drugs slow biological aging -- a landmark multi-drug design for the field.

title: Nature Feature: Partial Cellular Reprogramming About to Be Tested in Humans
description: Nature published an in-depth feature on the first human trial of partial epigenetic reprogramming via Life Biosciences' ER-100, treating glaucoma and NAION patients with three Yamanaka factors.

title: Life Biosciences' FDA-Cleared Trial: Is the FDA Warming to Rejuvenation?
description: Lifespan.io analyzes Life Biosciences' ER-100 trial and what FDA clearance means for the broader rejuvenation field, noting it could build regulatory trust in cellular reprogramming approaches.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences Phase 1 RLS-1496 Results: First GPX4 Modulator Shows Senolytic Efficacy in Humans
description: Rubedo's first-in-class GPX4 modulator RLS-1496 met its primary endpoint in Phase 1, with dose-dependent senescent cell reduction and clinical improvement in psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and photo-aged skin.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Multi-Phase Rapamycin Clinical Trial
description: NIA-funded study will evaluate rapamycin dosing, safety, and long-term effects in 84 older adults, with sub-studies on biological markers and optimal dosing strategies.

title: Over 700 South Texans to Test Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin for Aging
description: UT Health San Antonio's Barshop Institute is preparing one of the largest aging drug trials, testing three existing drugs -- rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin -- in over 700 older adults.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Congress: Aging Reframed as Loss of Biological Coordination
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity in Berlin (April 8-9) brought together researchers across mitochondria, microbiota, and senescence to reframe aging as a systems coordination problem.

title: Novel Gene Therapy Encourages Heart Regeneration via Atrial Natriuretic Peptide
description: RNA-lipid nanoparticles encoding Nppa in skeletal muscle produce pro-ANP converted to ANP by Corin in the heart, reducing scarring and improving cardiac function in animal models with a single injection.

title: High-Dose Influenza Vaccine Linked to Lower Alzheimer's Risk in Older Adults
description: Retrospective study of 120,775 adults aged 65+ found high-dose influenza vaccination associated with lower Alzheimer's risk in the first 25 months post-vaccination compared to standard-dose.

title: PANoptosis in Cardiac Aging: Targeting Multi-Modal Cell Death Shows Preclinical Promise
description: Programmed cell death integrating pyroptosis, apoptosis, and necroptosis via the PANoptosome complex offers a new preclinical target to delay cardiac aging.

title: Stem Cell Therapy Reverses Aging-Related Frailty in Phase 2b Trial
description: Longeveron's laromestrocel stem cell therapy showed 30.8% of treated patients improved to non-frail status in a 148-patient Phase 2b trial, with patients walking 63.4 meters farther at nine months.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment 2026: Set for a Breakout Year at $3.74B in Q1
description: Q1 2026 saw 49 financing events raising $3.74 billion, a 56% increase over Q1 2025, with a projected full-year range of $8-9 billion in longevity biotech investment.

title: Longevity Funding Tracker: 12 Pure-Play Deals Raise $530M+ Since Late 2025
description: Twelve longevity pure-players disclosed new funding since late 2025, led by Function Health's $298M Series B at $2.5B valuation, with deals spanning reprogramming, mTOR inhibitors, and diagnostics.

title: Gilead to Acquire Cancer Biotech Tubulis for Up to $5 Billion
description: Gilead Sciences will acquire German ADC specialist Tubulis for $3.15B upfront plus $1.85B in milestones, gaining next-generation antibody-drug conjugate technology for oncology.

title: The Senolytics Pipeline in 2026: Where the Science Still Looks Strong
description: Comprehensive analysis of the 2026 senolytics pipeline finds the field splitting into three lanes: academic D+Q combinations, localized precision therapies, and next-gen discovery including GPX4 modulation.

title: Rapamycin for Longevity: Comprehensive Review of Pros, Cons, and Future Perspectives
description: Extensive Frontiers review covering the PEARL trial, rapamycin's dose-dependent effects, and the critical finding that human data still lack proof of slowed biological aging despite strong preclinical evidence.

title: Blood Test Using piRNAs Predicts Two-Year Survival with 86% Accuracy
description: Duke and University of Minnesota researchers found six piRNAs in blood that predict two-year survival in older adults with 86% accuracy, outperforming age, cholesterol, and 180+ other clinical measures.

title: Human Trial Directly Compares NMN, NR, and Nicotinamide for NAD+ Boosting
description: Head-to-head human trial shows NMN and NR approximately double circulating NAD+ levels after 14 days, while nicotinamide only boosts NAD+ acutely. Gut bacteria play a key role in conversion.

title: Daily Multivitamin Slows Biological Aging by Four Months Over Two Years in RCT
description: Mass General Brigham-led RCT of nearly 1,000 older adults found daily multivitamin supplementation slowed biological aging across multiple epigenetic clocks, with greatest benefit in those biologically older than their chronological age.

title: Life Biosciences Secures First FDA Clearance for Epigenetic Reprogramming Human Trial
description: Life Biosciences, co-founded by David Sinclair, received FDA IND clearance for ER-100 to test partial epigenetic reprogramming in glaucoma and NAION patients, marking a first for the field.

title: Longevity Economy Projected to Reach $63B by 2035, Biohacking Goes Mainstream
description: LinkedIn's Big Ideas 2026 report projects the longevity economy at $63 billion by 2035, nearly triple its 2024 value, with Function Health, preventive diagnostics, and tech-driven wellness leading growth.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Labiotech profiles the top 11 anti-aging biotechs for 2026 including Altos Labs ($3B), Genflow Biosciences, Rejuvenate Bio, and others working on reprogramming, gene therapy, and regenerative medicine.

title: Bryan Johnson's Blueprint Protocol 2026 Update: Refined Diet, New Biomarkers, Rapamycin Dropped
description: Blueprint's 2026 update refines macros (2,250 cal, 130g protein), eliminates rapamycin due to side effects, and emphasizes measurement-first supplement decisions based on lab results.

title: Longevity Center Europe: Clients Aging 22% Slower Using DunedinPACE Measurements
description: Over 80 individuals at Longevity Center Europe tested at an average DunedinPACE of 0.78, meaning they age 22% slower than average, with at least 10 clients surpassing Bryan Johnson's reported 0.65.

title: Aryl Hydrocarbon Receptor Inhibition Promotes Axon Regeneration After Spinal Cord Injury
description: Published in Nature, AhR inhibition promotes axon regrowth in peripheral and spinal cord injury models via HIF1a, shifting cells from stress signaling to growth signaling.

title: Alex Zhavoronkov: Longevity Biotech Crossing from 'Interesting Thesis' to Mainstream
description: Insilico Medicine's Alex Zhavoronkov declares 2026 the breakout year for longevity biotech, with real translational science, clearer clinical pathways, and investors treating aging biology as serious risk-return.
